spec.description: Deep-dive JVM telemetry for all Java microservices and the Jenkins controller, instrumented via the OpenTelemetry Java agent (zero-code). Use this board to diagnose CPU saturation, heap pressure, GC overhead, thread contention, and HTTP latency degradation — with thresholds and legends pre-tuned for on-call triage.

spec.title: JVM Thread State
spec.description: Thread states stacked over time — runnable / waiting / timed_waiting / blocked. A growing **blocked** band = lock contention; a growing **waiting** band = thread-pool or queue starvation.

spec.title: Errors & Warnings — selected service
spec.description: App logs for $service_name filtered by $level (default error|warn). Click a line's trace_id to jump to the trace (logs→traces via the Loki datasource's derived fields).

spec.expr: {k8s_namespace_name=~"$namespace", service_name=~"$service_name"} | detected_level=~"${level:pipe}" | json | line_format "{{ if .message }}{{ .message }}{{ else if .msg }}{{ .msg }}{{ else }}{{ __line__ }}{{ end }}"

spec.title: Traces — selected service
spec.description: Recent traces for $service_name. Click a Trace ID → trace view, which links to logs (tracesToLogs) and metrics (tracesToMetrics) via the Tempo datasource config.

spec.title: JVM / runtime context (name · implementation · version · OTel agent)
spec.description: Per-service runtime context from target_info (resource attributes): JVM name/vendor/version, OS/arch, exec path, command args, OTel agent (distro) version.

options.content:
## CI-CD JVM Internals — how to read & use this board

Deep-dive JVM telemetry for all Java microservices and the Jenkins controller, exported via the **OpenTelemetry Java agent** (zero-code instrumentation). All metrics follow the [OpenTelemetry JVM Semantic Conventions](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/runtime/jvm-metrics/).

---

### What each section answers

| Section | Key question |
|---|---|
| **Overview** | Is anything in the red right now? CPU, heap %, live threads, and GC overhead at a glance |
| **Garbage Collection** | How much time is the JVM spending in GC? Are pauses bounded? Which collector (SerialGC vs G1) is running? |
| **Heap & Non-Heap** | How close is heap to its maximum? Is Metaspace or CodeHeap growing without bound? |
| **Memory Pools** | Which pool is filling — Eden, Tenured, or Metaspace? Is the live set **after GC** growing (leak indicator)? |
| **Buffers & advanced** | Are direct/mapped NIO buffers accumulating off-heap? (Requires experimental OTel telemetry.) |
| **HTTP response times** | Is GC pressure causing latency spikes? Which status codes drive the error rate? |
| **Logs & Traces** | What errors are firing, and what do recent traces look like for the selected service? |

---

### On-call triage flow

1. **Overview row** — scan the 6 KPI cards. Anything red? Start there.
2. **Heap Used % > 80 %** → open *JVM Heap* and *Memory Pool — live set after GC*. A rising Tenured/Old-gen floor between collections = slow memory leak.
3. **GC Time /s > 0.1** → check *GC Used Time* and *GC Pause p95/p99*. Pauses > 200 ms will show as latency spikes in the HTTP section.
4. **Latency spike** → align *HTTP latency p50/p95/p99* and *GC Pause p95/p99* timestamps. If they coincide, GC is the root cause; consider tuning heap size or switching to G1.
5. **High thread count or blocked threads** → *JVM Thread State* (stacked view). A growing `blocked` band = lock contention; a growing `waiting` band = thread-pool or queue starvation.
6. **5xx rate > 1 %** → jump to *Errors & Warnings* (Loki) and *Traces* (Tempo) for the affected service.

---

### Threshold reference

Threshold lines are drawn directly on the relevant charts — yellow = warning, red = critical.

| Signal | ⚠️ Yellow | 🔴 Red |
|---|---|---|
| CPU utilization (process & system) | 70 % | 90 % |
| GC Used Time | 0.1 s/s | 0.3 s/s |
| GC Pause p95/p99 | 200 ms | 500 ms |
| HTTP latency | 500 ms | 1 000 ms |
| Heap Used % (gauge) | 80 % | 92 % |
| HTTP 5xx error rate | 1 % | 5 % |
| Live Threads (stat) | 200 | 500 |

> **Note:** Heap Used (bytes) and Total Memory thresholds are rough defaults (512 MB / 1.5 GB and 1 GB / 2.5 GB). Adjust them to your actual `-Xmx` and container memory limits for accurate alerting.

spec.title: JVM CPU Used
spec.description: Process CPU utilization ratio over time. **Threshold lines at 70 % (yellow) and 90 % (red)**. Sustained > 70 % warrants tuning; > 90 % = CPU-bound, consider scaling or profiling.

spec.title: GC Used Time (per collector)
spec.description: GC time consumed per second by collector (SerialGC Copy/MarkSweep vs G1 Young/Old). **Threshold lines at 0.1 s/s (yellow) and 0.3 s/s (red)**. Approaching 1.0 s/s = GC-bound; increase heap or switch to G1/ZGC.

spec.title: GC Active (frequency)
spec.description: Collections/s by collector.

spec.title: GC Pause p95 / p99
spec.description: Stop-the-world pause distribution (p95 / p99) in **ms**. **Yellow ≥ 200 ms · Red ≥ 500 ms**. G1 keeps pauses bounded vs SerialGC. Correlate spikes with the *HTTP latency* panel — they will appear at the same timestamp.

spec.title: JVM Heap
spec.description: Heap memory over time: used vs committed vs max. The gap between committed and max is headroom before OOM. A used line approaching max without GC relief = heap exhaustion risk.

spec.title: JVM Non-Heap
spec.description: Metaspace + CodeHeap + Compressed Class (used vs committed).

spec.title: JVM CPU Load
spec.description: Recent process CPU utilization (0-1) vs the CPUs the JVM sees.

spec.title: JVM Memory Pool Used
spec.description: Used per pool (Eden/Survivor/Tenured/Metaspace/CodeHeap/Compressed Class).

spec.title: JVM Memory Pool — live set after GC
spec.description: Heap used per pool **immediately after a GC collection** — the true live set. A rising Tenured / Old-gen floor between GC cycles = objects are being promoted faster than they are collected = memory leak.

spec.title: Buffer Pools — memory used
spec.description: Off-heap direct and mapped NIO buffer pools (bytes in use). Needs the OTel agent’s experimental telemetry flag. Growing direct memory without a matching load increase = buffer leak (common in Netty-based services).

spec.title: Buffer Pools — count
spec.description: Number of buffers in each pool.

spec.title: CPU — process / system utilization + load avg
spec.description: Process CPU ratio + system CPU ratio + system load average 1 m. **Threshold lines at 70 % and 90 %** apply to the 0–1 ratio series. Load avg can exceed 1 on a busy multi-core host — compare to CPU count for saturation signal.

spec.title: HTTP latency p50 / p95 / p99
spec.description: Server-side request latency in **ms** (p50 / p95 / p99). **Threshold lines at 500 ms (yellow) and 1 000 ms (red)**. Correlate spikes with *GC Pause p95/p99* — co-located spikes = GC-induced latency.

spec.title: Request rate by status
spec.description: Throughput stacked by HTTP status code (2xx / 4xx / 5xx). The stacked view shows composition at a glance — a growing 5xx band means errors are rising relative to total traffic.

spec.title: HTTP 5xx error rate
spec.description: Percentage of requests returning 5xx.

spec.title: Heap Used
spec.description: Total heap used across selected services. **Yellow ≥ 512 MB · Red ≥ 1.5 GB** — adjust to your -Xmx. Use *Heap Used %* for a relative view that normalises across services with different heap configs.

spec.title: Total Memory Used
spec.description: Heap + non-heap (Metaspace, CodeHeap, Compressed Class, direct buffers). **Yellow ≥ 1 GB · Red ≥ 2.5 GB** — tune to your container memory limit. Divergence from heap alone = growing off-heap usage.

spec.title: Heap Used %
spec.description: Heap used / max (-XX:MaxRAMPercentage). >80% = GC pressure.

spec.title: Live Threads
spec.description: Live JVM thread count across selected services. **Yellow ≥ 200 · Red ≥ 500**. Sustained growth without a load increase = thread leak. See *JVM Thread State* for composition.

spec.title: GC Time /s
spec.description: Seconds spent in GC per second — a rate. Approaches 1.0 when the JVM is nearly GC-bound. Correlates directly with latency degradation and CPU steal visible in the CPU panels.

spec.title: JVM Class Loading
spec.description: Loaded classes (current) + load/unload churn.

spec.title: JVM Threads
spec.description: Live thread count split by daemon vs non-daemon over time. Legend shows **Last** and **Max**. Daemon threads are typically framework/GC threads; non-daemon threads control JVM shutdown.

spec.title: CI-CD JVM internals (all Java services + Jenkins)

spec.name: namespace
spec.label: Namespace (All incl. Jenkins)
spec.description: Tier: stable = namespace microservices, develop = microservices-develop. JVM/app metrics carry k8s_namespace_name (not deployment_environment), so namespace is the tier discriminator.

spec.name: level
spec.label: Log level
spec.description: Filter the logs panel by severity (detected_level). All = everything incl. plain-text (unknown).
